message:
fix: use functional setState updater in toggleAudio for atomic state sync

The previous toggleAudio implementation read audioEnabledRef.current,
negated it, then called both ref assignment and setState separately.
In React 19's concurrent mode, this pattern can cause the ref and
state to briefly diverge if a re-render interrupts between the two
assignments.

Refactored to use the functional updater form of setIsAudioEnabled:
the callback receives the previous state value, computes the new
state, updates the ref, and returns the new value — all within a
single atomic state transition. This guarantees the ref always
matches the committed state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
